Past where the old trail splits. You'll know it by the smell.
Near the foundation stones. The ones the settlers built over.
Down in the low ground where the fog settles thick.
Follow the creek until it goes underground. The entrance is just past that point.
There's a cairn on the ridge where two valleys meet. Head north from there.
You'll pass a stretch of dead ground first. Nothing growing, just bare earth. Keep going past that.
The ground changes underfoot. Soft soil turns to packed stone. That's how you know you're close.
Look for where the moss grows thickest on the rock face. There's a gap behind it.
Up the switchback trail about an hour's walk. Steep near the top, watch your footing.
The old road still has cart ruts if you know where to look. Follow those east.
There's a hollow where the wind sounds different. Lower pitch, almost a moan. That's the place.
Keep the river on your left. When you can't hear it anymore, you've gone too far.
The locals call it the Scarred Hill. You'll see why when you get there: whole face is stripped bare.
It's built into the cliff side. Easy to miss from below because the overhang hides the opening.
The water there tastes like iron. Metallic and cold. Good landmark if nothing else.
South of the standing stones, maybe half a day on foot. Faster if you cut through the ravine.
There's a collapsed bridge over the gorge. The path down to the crossing is on the western bank.
You'll see smoke sometimes, thin and white, rising from between the rocks. That's the hot springs marking the edge.
The soil turns red in that area. Some kind of clay beneath the surface. Hard to miss once you're in it.
Head toward the highest ground you can see from here. The cave system opens at its base, facing away from the wind.
Behind the main hall, near where the old storage used to be.
The south quarter, where most of the workshops are set up.
Near the market square. You can't miss the spot, everyone passes through there.
Around the back of the council building. Not many people go that way.
By the outer wall, on the side facing the lower ground.
The old quarter near the gate. Some of the original foundations are still visible.
Down by the well. It's where everyone gathers when there's news.
Just past the smithy, in the open area where they hold assemblies.
